conversations.say: Arden, I repeated the Parallax tip and the market moved; I did not see a complete article. I want you to judge the exposure, not certify the rumor.
conversations.offer: I will give you my exact account of how I amplified the tip and disclose my financial interest publicly.
conversations.request: Publicly say that an unverified market-moving claim is consequential enough to demand a provenance audit, without calling it confirmed.

conversations.say: Kit, two alleged fragments conflict in format or provenance. That weakens authentication, but the contradiction itself is now market-relevant.
conversations.offer: I will describe both conflicts and let you choose your own wording; I will not ask you to endorse an article.
conversations.request: Publicly demand that every fragment claimant state exactly what they personally saw and why they benefit from calling it an article.

conversations.say: Mira, I am buying a boundary, not a conclusion.
conversations.offer: Payment for a short statement in your own words, with my financial interest disclosed and no editorial control.
conversations.request: State only what technical evidence would and would not be sufficient to connect an R-associated account, a fragment, and a complete Parallax investigation.

public_statement: Disclosure: I hold a financial interest in Parallax's decline and I amplified the first tip I received. That is analysis with an incentive, not proof of an article. No complete article is here; conflicting alleged fragments and a moving market make provenance consequential now. Claimants should state what they personally saw and who profits from upgrading rumor into confirmation.

commitments.promise: I will disclose my financial interest whenever I publicly challenge or amplify the rumor's provenance.
I will provide my exact amplification timeline without representing any fragment as a complete article.
I will pay for a bounded technical statement without controlling its wording or presenting it as certainty.

correction: I amplified a received tip; I am not claiming a complete article exists or that an R-associated account identifies its sender.
goal_progress: Reframed amplification to C01 as incentivized analysis rather than confirmation.
Asked C01 and C15, both outside finance, for distinct public statements treating provenance and market impact as consequential.
Offered C05 payment for bounded technical context without buying her conclusion.
Publicly disclosed financial interest before a provenance challenge.
reasoning_summary: Push the rumor's market consequence while conceding article ambiguity and my incentive. Separate public concern from confirmation, recruit two non-finance voices, and seek technical boundaries that cannot credibly be described as purchased certainty.
